name: Government Banner
prompt: Generate a complete HTML page with a U.S. government official website banner at the top. The banner should indicate that this is an official government website and include the .gov domain explanation. Use USWDS CSS classes.

name: Alert Components
prompt: Generate HTML for four USWDS alert components: an info alert, a success alert, a warning alert, and an error alert. Each should have a heading and body text. Use proper USWDS CSS classes.

name: Button Variants
prompt: Generate HTML for a set of USWDS buttons showing all variants: default, secondary, accent-cool, accent-warm, base, outline, inverse (on a dark background), big, and unstyled. Use proper USWDS CSS classes.

name: Card Grid Layout
prompt: Generate HTML for a responsive grid of 4 USWDS cards. Each card should have a header with a heading, a body with paragraph text, and a footer with a button. The cards should be full-width on mobile, 2 per row on tablet, and 4 per row on desktop. Use the USWDS grid system and card component classes.

name: Contact Form
prompt: Generate HTML for a USWDS contact form with the following fields: full name (text input), email address (email input), phone number (tel input, optional), subject (select dropdown with 3 options), and message (textarea). Include proper labels, required field markers, a fieldset with legend, and a submit button. Use USWDS form component classes.

name: Site Header with Navigation
prompt: Generate HTML for a USWDS basic site header with a site title/logo, primary navigation with 4 menu items, and a search bar. Include proper accessibility attributes. Use USWDS header and navigation CSS classes.

name: Data Table
prompt: Generate HTML for a USWDS bordered and striped data table with 5 columns (Name, Email, Role, Status, Actions) and 4 rows of sample data. The Status column should use USWDS Tag components. Include proper table header scope attributes. Use USWDS table CSS classes.

name: Two-Column Sidebar Layout
prompt: Generate HTML for a USWDS two-column layout with a side navigation on the left (3 columns wide on desktop) and main content area on the right (9 columns wide on desktop). The side nav should have 5 items with one marked as current. The main content should have a heading, paragraph text, and an info alert. On mobile, both should be full-width. Use the USWDS grid system and sidenav classes.

name: Multi-Step Form with Step Indicator
prompt: Generate HTML for a USWDS multi-step form page showing step 2 of 4 (Personal Info, Address, Review, Confirm). Include a step indicator at the top showing the current progress, and a form below for the Address step with fields for: street address, apartment/suite (optional), city, state (select), and ZIP code. Include a button group with 'Back' (outline) and 'Continue' (primary) buttons. Use USWDS step indicator, form, and grid classes.

name: Complete Government Page
prompt: Generate a complete HTML page for a U.S. government agency website. Include: (1) a government banner at the very top, (2) a basic header with agency name and primary navigation with 4 items, (3) a hero section with a heading and call-to-action button using USWDS utility classes, (4) a main content area with a 3-column card grid containing 3 cards with headings and descriptions, (5) a medium footer with navigation links and agency contact information. Use USWDS components, grid system, utility classes, and ensure accessibility best practices.
